string.text: Revert every AlifePlus setting to its factory default. Takes effect immediately -- close and re-open MCM to see the updated sliders. Useful after experimenting or when upgrading to a new version with rebalanced defaults.
Event pipeline controls. Events pass through specialized rate limiters controlled by A-Life Ratio, A-Life Interval, Cause Budget, Consequence Budget.

Balance between events on the player's map and background maps, similar to X-Ray's original going speed. Higher values favor the player's map. Zero treats all maps equally. Negative values favor background maps.

[seconds]\nTime between radiant event evaluations. Lower means more activity, higher means a calmer Zone. Direct reactive events use a separate limiter with a fixed interval per event type, to accommodate bursty events like deaths.
Max reaction publishes per 60s (massacre, squadkill, basekill, wounded, harvest, alpha, alphakill).
Max stalker-needs publishes per 60s (hunger, sleep, rest, heal, shelter, money, supply, job, social).
Max mutant-instinct publishes per 60s (scatter, feed, sleep, explore, socialize, infest).
Max territorial-opportunity publishes per 60s (stash interactions, area conquest).
[count]\nMax activations per consequence type per 60 seconds. Prevents one consequence from firing too often.
[count]\nMax total consequence activations across all types per 60 seconds. Prevents consequence floods from multiple causes firing in quick succession.

When enabled, other mods can register squad ownership. AP will not touch squads owned by other mods (Warfare, BAO, etc). When disabled, AP ignores external ownership claims and controls all eligible squads.

PDA gossip composer. Zone events (kills, conquests, raids, mutant activity) are recorded as they happen, then periodically composed into radio chatter and delivered via PDA by an event-connected stalker.
Master toggle for the PDA news composer. When disabled, no gossip is dispatched. Event recording continues regardless.
[percent]\nProbability that a fired consequence is recorded for gossip. Lower = quieter Zone, higher = denser radio chatter. 100 records every consequence; 0 records nothing. Default: 20.
[seconds]\nMinimum delay between PDA messages. Each tick picks a random delay between min and max, so cadence feels human instead of mechanical.
[seconds]\nMaximum delay between PDA messages. Higher means a quieter Zone overall. Set min and max to the same value for fixed cadence.

Which faction radios you tune into. Own: only your faction speaks. Allies: your faction plus factions friendly to you. World: any non-enemy stalker. Secret faction channels (Monolith, Army, Greh, ISG) stay member-only regardless. Default: allies.
[game hours]\nHow old an event can be and still get gossiped about. Each compose tick picks a random event from the past N game hours. Lower = fresher news, higher = wider gossip pool. Default: 12.
Cause: Alpha\nDesc: Mutant accumulated enough kills to level up as an alpha. 10 levels, each requiring more kills. Stalkers use engine rank instead.\nRules: kill threshold
Fire cause:alpha when a mutant reaches a new alpha level.
Kills required per alpha level. At 2: level 1 at 2 kills, level 10 at 20 kills.
Cause: Alphakill\nDesc: An alpha mutant has died.\nRules: alpha died
Fire cause:alphakill event when alpha mutants die. Required for targeted pack hunt.
Cause: Massacre\nDesc: Deaths pile up at a smart terrain.\nRules: multiple human deaths; not at base
Track death accumulation and fire massacre events when threshold reached.
Deaths required in area before massacre event fires.
[seconds]\nTime window for death accumulation. Deaths older than this don't count.

Cause: Stash Loot Available\nDesc: Squad finds a non-empty stash and loots it.\nRules: eye range; active period; loot-seeking stalkers; personality(greed, perception); stash non-empty; not at base; MVT
Cause: Stash Ambush Available\nDesc: Outlaw squad stakes out a heavily-stocked stash.\nRules: eye range; active period; outlaw stalkers; personality(greed, aggression); items >= min; not at base; MVT
Cause: Stash Fill Available\nDesc: Squad finds an empty stash and stocks it.\nRules: eye range; active period; any stalker; personality(greed, relation); stash empty; not at base; MVT
Game hours per squad before another loot attempt. MVT recovery time.
Game hours per squad before another ambush attempt. MVT recovery time.
Game hours per squad before another fill attempt. MVT recovery time.
Minimum item count for outlaws to stake out a stash. Below this, they loot directly instead.
Cause: Squadkill\nDesc: A squad has been wiped out.\nRules: human squad wiped; not at base
Fire cause:squadkill event when last squad member dies.
Cause: Basekill\nDesc: Deaths pile up at a faction base.\nRules: multiple human deaths; at base
Track deaths at faction bases and fire events when threshold reached.
Deaths required at base before event fires.
[seconds]\nTime window for death accumulation at base.
Cause: Wounded\nDesc: An NPC or player used a healing item.\nRules: healing item used; not at base
Fire event when NPC or player uses healing item.
Cause: Area Conquer Available\nDesc: Stalker squad claims an empty smart.\nRules: eye range; active period; non-ecologist stalkers; personality(territory, aggression); empty smart; not at base; MVT
Cause: Area Swarm Available\nDesc: Mutant squad claims an empty smart.\nRules: eye range; active period; non-cowardly mutants; personality(territory, aggression); empty smart; not at base; MVT

Cause: Area Infest Available\nDesc: Mutant claims a lair or surge shelter.\nRules: eye range; active period; non-cowardly mutants; personality(territory, survival); species-specific shelter; not at base; not infested; has alpha; per-level cap; MVT
Game hours per squad before another conquer attempt. MVT recovery time.
Game hours per squad before another swarm attempt. MVT recovery time.
Game hours per squad before another infest attempt. MVT recovery time.
Cause: Harvest\nDesc: An NPC or player picked up an artefact.\nRules: artefact picked up
Fire cause:harvest when artefact is picked up.
Cause: Hunger Campfire Available\nDesc: Hungry squad heads to a campfire to eat.\nRules: radio range; active period; any stalker; personality(survival); campfire; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Sleep Campfire Available\nDesc: Tired squad beds down at a campfire.\nRules: radio range; dormant period; any stalker; personality(survival); campfire; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Rest Campfire Available\nDesc: Worn squad takes a halt at a campfire.\nRules: radio range; dormant period; any stalker; personality(survival); campfire; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Heal Shelter Available\nDesc: Wounded squad seeks a surge shelter.\nRules: radio range; active period; any stalker; personality(territory, survival); surge shelter; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Shelter Indoor Available\nDesc: Squad takes an indoor surge shelter.\nRules: radio range; dormant period; any stalker; personality(territory, discipline); surge shelter; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Shelter Outdoor Available\nDesc: Squad takes a campfire as outdoor shelter.\nRules: radio range; dormant period; any stalker; personality(perception); campfire; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Supply Trader Available\nDesc: Squad heads to a trader to restock.\nRules: radio range; active period; any stalker; personality(greed, relation); trader; Hull
Cause: Money Harvest Available\nDesc: Squad heads to an anomaly to pull artefacts.\nRules: radio range; active period; self-serving stalkers; personality(greed, perception); anomaly; Hull
Cause: Money Hunt Available\nDesc: Squad heads to a lair to hunt mutants.\nRules: radio range; active period; naturalist stalkers; personality(greed, perception); lair; Hull

Cause: Job Outpost Available\nDesc: Squad mans a remote post on watch.\nRules: radio range; active period; principled stalkers; personality(territory, discipline); not at base; not at lair; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Job Explore Available\nDesc: Squad scouts an unclaimed smart.\nRules: radio range; active period; self-serving stalkers; personality(perception, territory); unclaimed; Hull
Cause: Job Research Available\nDesc: Ecologist squad samples an anomaly field.\nRules: radio range; active period; ecologists; personality(perception, discipline); anomaly; Hull
Cause: Social Campfire Available\nDesc: Squad gathers at a campfire to drink.\nRules: radio range; active period; any stalker; personality(relation); campfire; non-hostile; Hull
Cause: Social Base Available\nDesc: Squad returns to base for downtime.\nRules: radio range; active period; principled stalkers; personality(relation, territory); base; non-hostile; Hull
[game-hours]\nTime between rest events (smoke, drink, relax). Default 4.
[game-hours]\nTime between supply events (trader visit, stash deposit). Default 48.
[game-hours]\nTime between job events (guard, explore, research). Default 18.
[game-hours]\nTime between social events (campfire gathering, base visit). Default 36.
Desc: Registers a new alpha or raises its level\nCause: Alpha\nRules: mutant; kill threshold
Register mutants as alphas when they level up from kills.
Maximum number of alphas allowed worldwide. Only blocks new alphas, not level-ups.
Alpha mutants carry valuable items (mutant parts, hides, artefacts). Better loot at higher levels. Items persist in inventory and drop on death.
Desc: Same-species mutants chase the killer\nCause: Alphakill\nRules: scent range; same species; personality(aggression)
Same-species mutants on the level pursue the killer.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum alpha squads that pursue the killer.
Maximum times a hunter re-scripts to follow a fleeing killer. 0 disables chase.
Desc: Cowardly mutants scavenge the corpses\nCause: Massacre\nRules: scent range; cowardly mutants; personality(survival, perception)
Outlaws and cowardly mutants converge on massacre sites.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum scavenger squads that respond to a massacre.
Desc: Victim-faction stalkers head to the kill site\nCause: Massacre\nRules: radio range; non-renegade stalkers; personality(perception, relation)
Stalker factions investigate when their own die.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum faction squads that investigate a massacre (random 1..N).
Desc: Outlaws strip the corpses\nCause: Massacre\nRules: radio range; outlaw stalkers; personality(greed, perception)
Outlaws strip corpses at massacre sites.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum outlaw squads that walk to loot a massacre.
Desc: Squad arrives and waits to ambush a looter\nCause: Stash Ambush Available
Bandits camp at revealed stash locations and wait.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad arrives and loots the stash\nCause: Stash Loot Available
Scavengers race to revealed stashes to loot them before player arrives.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
When enabled, NPCs skip stashes containing toolkits, repair kits, craft tools, or workshop kits. The entire stash is left untouched.
Desc: Squad arrives and stocks the empty stash\nCause: Stash Fill Available
Stalkers hide supplies in stashes they discover.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Minimum items to hide in stash.
Maximum items to hide in stash.
Desc: Victim-faction squads chase the killer\nCause: Squadkill\nRules: radio range; unprincipled or outlaw stalkers; personality(aggression, relation)
Nearby friendly squads pursue killers when squad is wiped.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum avenger squads that pursue a killer (random 1..N).
Maximum times an avenger re-scripts to follow a fleeing killer. 0 disables chase.
Desc: Victim-faction squads flee to nearest base\nCause: Squadkill\nRules: radio range; non-principled stalkers; personality(inv_discipline, inv_aggression); base on level
Squads retreat to safety when nearby friendlies are eliminated.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum squads that flee per event (random 1..N).
Desc: Victim-faction squads rush to defend the base\nCause: Basekill\nRules: radio range; non-renegade stalkers; personality(discipline, relation)
Nearby squads rush to reinforce base under attack.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum reinforcement squads sent to defend base (random 1..N).
Desc: Nearby squads flee to a distant friendly base\nCause: Basekill\nRules: eye range; non-principled stalkers; personality(inv_discipline, inv_territory); base on level
Squads abandon base under heavy attack and retreat to safety.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum squads that flee per event (random 1..N).
Desc: Predator and aberrant mutants converge on the wounded\nCause: Wounded\nRules: radio range; predator or aberrant species; personality(aggression, perception)
Predator and aberrant mutants hunt wounded.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum mutant squads drawn to wounded (random 1..N).
Desc: Same-faction medics converge on the wounded\nCause: Wounded\nRules: radio range; non-outlaw stalkers; personality(relation, discipline)
Faction members rush to assist wounded comrades.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum helper squads sent per event (random 1..N).
Desc: Stalker squad arrives and adds its faction to the spawn pool\nCause: Area Conquer Available
Dispatch human squads to claim empty areas.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Mutant pack arrives and adds its species to the spawn pool\nCause: Area Swarm Available
Dispatch mutant squads to claim empty areas.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Outlaws chase the artefact taker\nCause: Harvest\nRules: radio range; outlaw stalkers; personality(greed, aggression)
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum hunter squads that pursue the artefact taker (random 1..N).
Maximum times a hunter re-scripts to follow a fleeing taker. 0 disables chase.
Desc: Aberrant mutants haunt the pickup site\nCause: Harvest\nRules: scent range; aberrant species; personality(perception, territory)
Aberrant mutants converge on artefact pickup sites.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum aberrant squads that converge on the pickup site (random 1..N).
Desc: Squad heads to a campfire to eat\nCause: Hunger Campfire Available
Hungry stalkers seek campfires to eat.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC eats food from inventory: bread, sausage, canned goods, vodka, beer. Item is removed.
Desc: Squad heads to a campfire to sleep\nCause: Sleep Campfire Available
Tired stalkers seek campfires to sleep at night.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to a campfire to rest\nCause: Rest Campfire Available
Fatigued stalkers seek campfires to rest.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC smokes a cigarette or drinks vodka/beer from inventory. Item is removed.
Desc: Squad heads to a shelter to heal\nCause: Heal Shelter Available
Wounded stalkers seek sheltered locations for healing.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC uses a medkit, bandage, or stimpack from inventory. Item is removed.
Desc: Squad takes indoor shelter from the surge\nCause: Shelter Indoor Available
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad takes outdoor shelter from the surge\nCause: Shelter Outdoor Available
Exposed stalkers seek campfires when no surge shelter is nearby.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to an anomaly field to harvest artefacts\nCause: Money Harvest Available
Stalkers seek anomaly zones when they need money.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to a lair to hunt for trophies\nCause: Money Hunt Available
Stalkers hunt mutants when they need money.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to a trader to sell artefacts and resupply\nCause: Supply Trader Available
Stalkers visit traders when they need supplies.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC sells one artefact from inventory and receives ammo, grenades, or medical supplies. Both items are real.
Desc: Squad heads to an outpost to guard\nCause: Job Outpost Available
Stalkers guard outposts and checkpoints when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC smokes a cigarette from inventory while on guard duty. Item is removed.
Desc: Squad heads to unclaimed territory to scout\nCause: Job Explore Available
Stalkers explore unknown areas when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to an anomaly field to research\nCause: Job Research Available
Stalkers research anomaly zones when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Desc: Squad heads to a campfire to socialize\nCause: Social Campfire Available
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC shares a cigarette or drink from inventory at the campfire. Item is removed.
Desc: Squad heads to a base to socialize\nCause: Social Base Available
Lonely stalkers return to base for company.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC shares a cigarette or drink from inventory at base. Item is removed.
Combat modifiers for alpha mutants. Alphas are mutants that earned their status through kills. Hit power bonus makes their attacks land harder. Hit power reduction makes incoming hits land softer.
Alpha mutant attacks land harder. Scales with alpha level.
Hit power bonus per alpha level. At 0.05: level 1 = +5%, level 10 = +50%.
Incoming hits against alpha mutants land softer. Scales with alpha level.
Hit power reduction per alpha level. At 0.05: level 1 = -5%, level 10 = -50%.
Alpha mutants never panic or flee. Panic threshold set to zero on first hit.
Combat modifiers for veteran+ stalkers (rank 12000+). Based on engine rank earned through kills. Scales linearly from veteran to legend.
Veteran+ stalker attacks land harder. A legend at max bonus hits significantly harder.
Maximum hit power bonus at legend rank. At 0.15: veteran = +3%, legend = +15%.
Incoming hits against veteran+ stalkers land softer. A legend at max reduction takes significantly less.
Maximum hit power reduction at legend rank. At 0.15: veteran = -3%, legend = -15%.

Smart terrain mutation for area conquest. Adds an extra respawn slot for the conquering faction alongside the original LTX spawns. Squads from both the conqueror and the original occupants compete for population slots. Disable to keep the squad movement (the area_conquer consequence) without touching engine spawn tables.
Conquered areas spawn the conqueror's squads alongside original spawns. Disable to keep squad movement without spawn changes.
Maximum smart terrains that can be conquered. FIFO eviction when reached.
Extra squads at conquered area. Added alongside original spawns.
Conquered smart terrains lose the conqueror's spawns after this many game hours. Original spawns are unaffected. Set to 0 to disable decay.

Smart terrain mutation for mutant infestation. Replaces original LTX spawns with the infesting species exclusively. Per-level cap limits how many smarts can be infested on each level. Disable to keep the squad movement (the area_infest consequence) without touching engine spawn tables.
Infested areas spawn the infesting species exclusively, replacing original spawns. Disable to keep squad movement without spawn changes.
[1-5]\nMaximum number of infested smart terrains per level. Default: 1.
[1-5]\nNumber of squads the infested spawn entry can produce. Default: 3.
[0-168]\nGame hours before infestation expires. 0 = permanent (no decay). Default: 0.
Debug and development. DEBUG enables tracing, performance timing, and verbose logs. Debug markers show squad destinations on the PDA map.
Controls log verbosity and debug features. DEBUG enables OTel-style tracing, performance timing, and verbose logs. Has performance cost.
Show squad destination markers on the PDA map. Approximate positions for tracing A-Life movement, not exact. Reload save to apply.
Show pipeline statistics on screen and write to log every 10 seconds. Select position or Off. Zero overhead when disabled.
Cause: Scatter Available\nDesc: Mutant flees a higher-tier threat.\nRules: scent range; any period; prey mutants; personality(INV_aggression, survival); threat nearby; safe smart; not at base; Hull
Cause: Feed Available\nDesc: Mutant goes to a field to feed.\nRules: scent range; active period; any mutant; personality(survival, perception); territory; not at base; Hull
Cause: Slumber Field Available\nDesc: Mutant sleeps in the open.\nRules: scent range; dormant period; cowardly mutants; personality(survival); territory; not at base; Hull
Cause: Slumber Lair Available\nDesc: Mutant retreats to its lair.\nRules: scent range; dormant period; feral or predator; personality(survival); lair; not at base; Hull
Cause: Slumber Surge Available\nDesc: Mutant takes shelter from anomalies.\nRules: scent range; dormant period; aberrant or predator; personality(survival); surge shelter; not at base; Hull
Cause: Roam Available\nDesc: Mutant wanders to new ground.\nRules: scent range; active period; non-aberrant mutants; personality(perception, territory); territory or lair; not at base; Hull
Cause: Pack Available\nDesc: Mutant joins its kin nearby.\nRules: scent range; active period; feral or predator; personality(relation); kin present; not at base; Hull
[game-hours]\nHull recovery time for the scatter drive. Default 12.
[game-hours]\nHull recovery time for the feed drive. Default 12.
[game-hours]\nHull recovery time for the slumber drive (shared by all 3 answers; any answer firing resets). Default 12.
[game-hours]\nHull recovery time for the roam drive. Default 12.
[game-hours]\nHull recovery time for the pack drive. Default 24.
Desc: Pack scatters from a higher-tier predator\nCause: Scatter Available
Route lower-tier mutants away from nearby higher-tier predators to a safe smart terrain.
Mutants sprint when scattering from predators.
Desc: Pack heads to a hunting ground to feed\nCause: Feed Available
Route mutants to open territory for feeding.
Desc: Cowardly pack curls up in territory to sleep\nCause: Slumber Field Available
Route cowardly mutants to open territory smarts.
Desc: Feral or predator pack dens up in a lair to sleep\nCause: Slumber Lair Available
Route feral mutants and predators to lair smarts.
Desc: Predator or aberrant pack takes shelter from the surge\nCause: Slumber Surge Available
Route aberrant mutants and predators to surge shelters.
Desc: Pack roams unclaimed territory\nCause: Roam Available
Route mutants to nearby territory or lair for exploration.
Desc: Pack converges with kin\nCause: Pack Available
Desc: Mutant pack arrives and replaces the spawn pool with its species\nCause: Area Infest Available
Enable the area infest consequence. Mutants will claim smart terrains as nests.
When enabled, squads move at rush speed to claim their nest.
